notes: Batch 17-E: 65 entries. Decisions: (1) All 19 app/src/remote_server/server_model.rs entries flagged telemetry_payload — they are proto error.message field strings sent over the wire. Verified one ('Cannot index remote codebase because the maximum number of codebase indexes has been reached.') is substring-matched in app/src/settings_view/code_page.rs (REMOTE_CODEBASE_INDEX_LIMIT_REACHED_FAILURE), and others flow into safe_error! sinks; per PRD policy on server-side code and protocol-key behaviour, all flagged. (2) All 18 crates/remote_server/src/client/mod.rs entries are safe_warn!/safe_error! macro args (defined in warp_core/src/safe_log.rs as wrappers over log::warn!/log::error!) — pure telemetry, flagged. (3) All 15 app/src/terminal/ssh/error.rs entries are UI text: button labels (Warpify without TMUX, Continue without Warpification), error description/title constants rendered in description_row + render_title_ui, and the GitHub-issue title fragment used in URL — translated. The trailing/leading spaces in 'We are actively working on...' and ' on GitHub...' preserved verbatim (they sandwich a hyperlink). (4) share_block_modal.rs: 4 .expect() panic strings (lines 441, 1252, 1465, 1471 + the new 1471 entry 'Block should exist in model') flagged panic_message; 9 UI labels translated (Command and Output, Creating block..., Create link, Get embed, Manage shared blocks, Share block, embedded warp block screen-reader title, etc.). 'block' rendered as '命令块' per glossary; Warp/SSH/TMUX kept English. ASCII '...' converted to '……'.
